steps.title: Identify the claim
steps.content: Toomer uses figurative language to connect the urban DC environment with the rural South of the narrator's past. The right quote must use figurative language and link both settings.

steps.title: Match the quote
steps.content:
D: "when the wind is from the South, soil of my homeland falls like a fertile shower upon the lean streets of [Washington, DC]" — a simile ("like a fertile shower") that ties Southern soil/homeland directly to DC's streets. Both settings appear, joined by figurative imagery.

A only describes train sounds in DC. B mentions singing but not the urban/rural link. C describes DC street trees but never invokes the South.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
